rows.Verbatim Snippet: If accounting and other records are kept by the company at a place outside Singapore there must be sent to and kept at a place in Singapore
rows.Mapping Rationale: This section requires companies to keep copies of certain records in Singapore when records are kept abroad. Maps to P6-I2 because it imposes a domestic storage requirement for a copy while allowing the original records to remain outside Singapore.
rows.Notes: Discovery: master dataset already records s. 199 of this law. Modality: must; exceptions: none

rows.Verbatim Snippet: The organisation must ensure that the copy of the personal data it preserves for the purposes of subsection (1) is a complete and accurate copy of the personal data concerned.
rows.Mapping Rationale: This section requires organisations to preserve a copy of personal data. It maps to P6-I2 because the legal function is domestic copy retention, i.e., a local-storage requirement for personal data.
rows.Notes: Discovery: instrument is known but s. 22A is not among its recorded provisions (['11', '25', '26']). Modality: must; exceptions: none

rows.Verbatim Snippet: the organisation must preserve, for not less than the prescribed period, a copy of the personal data concerned.
rows.Mapping Rationale: This section requires preservation of a copy of personal data. Maps to P6-I2 because it imposes domestic storage of a copy of personal data, while transfer is not prohibited.
rows.Notes: Discovery: instrument is known but s. 22A is not among its recorded provisions (['11', '25', '26']). Modality: must; exceptions: none

rows.Verbatim Snippet: An organisation must not transfer any personal data to a country or territory outside Singapore except in accordance with requirements prescribed under this Act
rows.Mapping Rationale: This section prohibits transfer of personal data abroad unless prescribed requirements are met. Maps to P6-I4 because it is a cross-border data-flow condition: transfer is permitted if comparable-protection requirements are satisfied.
rows.Notes: Discovery: master dataset already records s. 26 of this law. Modality: must not; exceptions: except in accordance with prescribed requirements under this Act

rows.Verbatim Snippet: If an individual gives, or is deemed to have given, consent to the disclosure of personal data about the individual by one organisation to another organisation for a particular purpose, the individual is deemed to consent to the collection, use or disclosure of the personal data for that particular purpose by that other organisation.
rows.Mapping Rationale: This section establishes deemed consent for collection, use or disclosure. Maps to P6-I4 because cross-organisation personal-data transfer is permitted if consent is given/deemed given, i.e. a conditional flow regime rather than a ban.
rows.Notes: Discovery: instrument is known but s. 15 is not among its recorded provisions (['11', '25', '26']). Modality: deemed consent / permits; exceptions: none

rows.Verbatim Snippet: This Act does not apply in respect of — ( a ) personal data about an individual that is contained in a record that has been in existence for at least 100 years; or ( b ) personal data about a deceased individual
rows.Mapping Rationale: This provision establishes scope exclusions for certain personal data. Maps to P7-I1 because it shows the Act is not purely sectoral and is the economy's horizontal personal-data-protection framework, even though it has carve-outs.
rows.Notes: Discovery: instrument is known but s. 4 is not among its recorded provisions (['11', '25', '26']). Modality: does not apply; exceptions: for personal data about an individual who has been dead for 10 years or less, disclosure provisions and section 24 still apply

rows.Verbatim Snippet: the technical or other standards relating to cybersecurity to be maintained in respect of a provider-owned critical information infrastructure, third-party-owned critical information infrastructure, system of temporary cybersecurity concern
rows.Mapping Rationale: This section permits regulations establishing cybersecurity standards and incident/reporting controls for critical infrastructure. Maps to P7-I2 because it is a dedicated cybersecurity framework (horizontal for covered CII categories), so the economy does not lack such a framework.
rows.Notes: Discovery: instrument is known but s. 48 is not among its recorded provisions (none recorded). Modality: may make regulations; exceptions: none

rows.Verbatim Snippet: The owner of a provider-owned critical information infrastructure must establish such mechanisms and processes for the purposes of detecting cybersecurity threats and incidents
rows.Mapping Rationale: This section requires owners of provider-owned critical information infrastructure to establish detection mechanisms and processes. Maps to P7-I2 because it is a dedicated cybersecurity duty covering incident detection for critical information infrastructure, part of a horizontal cybersecurity frame
rows.Notes: Discovery: instrument is known but s. 14 is not among its recorded provisions (none recorded). Modality: must; exceptions: none

rows.Verbatim Snippet: must obtain a legally binding commitment from the owner of the third‑party‑owned critical information infrastructure
rows.Mapping Rationale: This section requires a designated provider to obtain a binding commitment for incident notification. It maps to P7-I2 because it is a cybersecurity incident-reporting duty within the Cybersecurity Act, part of a dedicated horizontal cybersecurity framework.
rows.Notes: Discovery: instrument is known but s. 16I is not among its recorded provisions (none recorded). Modality: must; exceptions: none

rows.Verbatim Snippet: A designated provider responsible for third‑party‑owned critical information infrastructure must obtain a legally binding commitment from the owner of the third‑party‑owned critical information infrastructure
rows.Mapping Rationale: This section requires designated providers to obtain a binding cybersecurity commitment for third-party-owned CII. Maps to P7-I2 because it is a sectoral cybersecurity obligation, not a horizontal dedicated framework.
rows.Notes: Discovery: instrument is known but s. 16F is not among its recorded provisions (none recorded). Modality: must; exceptions: none

rows.Verbatim Snippet: direct, by written notice, any person to carry out such remedial measures, or to cease carrying on such activities
rows.Mapping Rationale: This section permits incident-response authorities to direct any person to take remedial measures or stop activities. Maps to P7-I2 because it establishes a horizontal incident-response/cybersecurity enforcement framework, not a lack of one.
rows.Notes: Discovery: instrument is known but s. 20 is not among its recorded provisions (none recorded). Modality: direct, by written notice; exceptions: none

rows.Verbatim Snippet: the Commissioner may exercise, or may authorise the Deputy Commissioner, an Assistant Commissioner, a cybersecurity officer or an authorised officer to exercise, such of the powers mentioned in subsection (2) as are necessary to investigate the cybersecurity threat or incident
rows.Mapping Rationale: This section permits the Commissioner and authorized officers to investigate and prevent cybersecurity threats/incidents. Maps to P7-I2 because it establishes a dedicated horizontal cybersecurity response framework for incident investigation/prevention.
rows.Notes: Discovery: instrument is known but s. 20 is not among its recorded provisions (none recorded). Modality: may; exceptions: none

rows.Verbatim Snippet: ( aa ) compliance with any prescribed technical or other standards relating to cybersecurity in respect of the provider‑owned critical information infrastructure;
rows.Mapping Rationale: This section permits the Commissioner to issue directions requiring compliance with cybersecurity standards for provider-owned critical information infrastructure. Maps to P7-I2 because it shows a dedicated sectoral cybersecurity framework, not a horizontal one.
rows.Notes: Discovery: instrument is known but s. 12 is not among its recorded provisions (none recorded). Modality: may; exceptions: none

rows.Verbatim Snippet: An employer must make, and keep for the period prescribed (called in this section the record retention period), employee records
rows.Mapping Rationale: This section requires employers to retain employee records for a prescribed period. Maps to P7-I3 because it establishes a minimum retention requirement tied to a specified period, even though the exact duration is set elsewhere.
rows.Notes: Discovery: master dataset already records s. 95 of this law. Modality: must; exceptions: none

rows.Verbatim Snippet: The company must retain the records referred to in subsection (1) for a period of not less than 5 years
rows.Mapping Rationale: This section requires companies to retain accounting records. Maps to P7-I3 because it establishes a minimum retention period (not less than 5 years).
rows.Notes: Discovery: master dataset already records s. 199 of this law. Modality: must; exceptions: none

rows.Verbatim Snippet: the organisation must preserve, for not less than the prescribed period, a copy of the personal data concerned.

rows.Notes: Discovery: instrument is known but s. 22A is not among its recorded provisions (['11', '25', '26']). Modality: must; exceptions: none

rows.Verbatim Snippet: must retain in safe custody transfer pricing documentation prepared by the person for each transaction, for a period of at least 5 years
rows.Mapping Rationale: This section requires retention of transfer pricing documentation. Maps to P7-I3 because it clearly sets a minimum retention period of at least 5 years.
rows.Notes: Discovery: instrument is known but s. 34F is not among its recorded provisions (['67']). Modality: must; exceptions: none

rows.Verbatim Snippet: retain every such record for a period of not less than 3 years after the date of the occasion to which the record relates
rows.Mapping Rationale: This section requires a licensee to retain records. Maps to P7-I3 because it sets a minimum retention period of not less than 3 years, satisfying the keep>=X legal test.
rows.Notes: Discovery: instrument is known but s. 29 is not among its recorded provisions (none recorded). Modality: must; exceptions: none

rows.Verbatim Snippet: a person who was an officer of the company immediately before the company was dissolved must ensure that all books and papers of the company are retained for a period of at least 5 years after the date on which the company was dissolved
rows.Mapping Rationale: This section requires retention of books and papers. Maps to P7-I3 because it establishes a minimum retention period of at least 5 years.
rows.Notes: Discovery: instrument is known but s. 344H is not among its recorded provisions (['145', '199', '215AA', '368', '370', '4']). Modality: must ensure; exceptions: none

rows.Verbatim Snippet: A licence is in force for such period (not exceeding 5 years) as the licensing officer may specify in the licence, starting from the date of its issue.
rows.Mapping Rationale: This provision establishes the licence duration. Maps to P7-I3 because it requires an in-force period of a specified length (up to 5 years), i.e., a retention/validity minimum period is set by the licence term.

rows.Verbatim Snippet: An organisation must designate one or more individuals to be responsible for ensuring that the organisation complies with this Act.
rows.Mapping Rationale: This section requires organisations to appoint responsible individuals. Maps to P7-I4 because it creates a horizontal accountability officer function (DPO-like designation) across organisations, not merely a privacy rule in general.
rows.Notes: Discovery: master dataset already records s. 11 of this law. Modality: must; exceptions: none

rows.Verbatim Snippet: a police officer or an authorised person investigating an arrestable offence may, at any time — ( a ) access, inspect and check the operation in or from Singapore of a computer
rows.Mapping Rationale: This section permits police/authorised persons to access and inspect computers. Maps to P7-I5 because it authorizes government access to personal data without any requirement for independent judicial authorization.
rows.Notes: Discovery: master dataset already records s. 39 of this law. Modality: may; exceptions: Subject to subsection (1A)

rows.Verbatim Snippet: The police officer or authorised person mentioned in subsection (1) is entitled to — ( a ) access any information, code or technology which has the capability of retransforming or unscrambling encrypted data into readable and comprehensible format or text
rows.Mapping Rationale: This section permits police access to decryption information. Maps to P7-I5 because it allows government access to personal data without independent judicial authorization; no court order or warrant is required.
rows.Notes: Discovery: instrument is known but s. 40 is not among its recorded provisions (['20', '39']). Modality: is entitled to; exceptions: none

rows.Verbatim Snippet: the police officer or the Chief Executive or the employee of the Authority may, if he or she has reasonable grounds for believing that by reason of the delay in obtaining a search warrant the article is likely to be removed
rows.Mapping Rationale: This section permits warrantless search and seizure by specified officials. Maps to P7-I5 because it authorizes government access to personal data without prior independent judicial authorization where delay in obtaining a warrant risks removal of the article/document.

rows.Verbatim Snippet: a data user may transfer any personal data to a place outside Malaysia if— (a) the data subject has given his consent to the transfer;
rows.Mapping Rationale: This section permits cross-border transfer of personal data. Maps to P6-I4 because transfer is allowed if specified conditions are met (e.g., consent, contract, legal necessity), so it is a conditional flow regime, not a ban.
rows.Notes: Discovery: master dataset already records s. 129 of this law. Modality: may; exceptions: none

rows.Verbatim Snippet: A data user shall not transfer any personal data of a data subject to a place outside Malaysia unless to such place as specified by the Minister, upon the recommendation of the Commissioner, by notification published in the Gazette.
rows.Mapping Rationale: This section prohibits cross-border transfer of personal data unless the destination is officially specified. Maps to P6-I4 because transfer is permitted if the stated approval/notification condition is met, so it is a conditional flow regime, not a ban.
rows.Notes: Discovery: master dataset already records s. 129 of this law. Modality: shall not transfer unless; exceptions: unless to such place as specified by the Minister, upon the recommendation of the Commissioner, by notification published in the Gazette

rows.Verbatim Snippet: the Minister may specify any place outside Malaysia if— (a) there is in that place in force any law which is substantially similar to this Act, or that serves the same purposes as this Act; or (b) that place ensures an adequate level of protection
rows.Mapping Rationale: This section permits cross-border transfer designation. Maps to P6-I4 because it allows personal data to be transferred to a specified foreign place if adequacy/similar-law conditions are met, i.e., a conditional flow regime.
rows.Notes: Discovery: master dataset already records s. 129 of this law. Modality: may specify; exceptions: none

rows.Verbatim Snippet: the data user shall cease to transfer any personal data of a data subject to such place with effect from the time as specified by the Minister in the notification.
rows.Mapping Rationale: This section requires cessation of transfers to a place once the Minister’s notification is amended/cancelled. Maps to P6-I4 because cross-border transfer remains permitted to listed places, but only while the statutory condition (adequate foreign law/notification) is met.
rows.Notes: Discovery: master dataset already records s. 129 of this law. Modality: shall cease; exceptions: If the Commissioner recommends and the Minister cancels or amends the notification, the place ceases to be a permitted destination and transfers must stop.

rows.Verbatim Snippet: A data user shall not— (a) in the case of personal data other than sensitive personal data, process personal data about a data subject unless the data subject has given his consent to the processing of the personal data; or (b) in the case of sensitive personal data, process sensitive personal data about a data subject except in accordance with the provisions of section 40.
rows.Mapping Rationale: This section prohibits processing personal data unless consent is given, with a separate lawful path for sensitive data under section 40. It maps to P6-I4 because it creates a conditional permission regime for data handling, though not specifically cross-border transfer.
rows.Notes: Discovery: master dataset already records s. 6 of this law. Modality: shall not; exceptions: non-sensitive personal data may be processed if consent is given; sensitive personal data may be processed if section 40 is complied with

rows.Verbatim Snippet: no personal data shall, without the consent of the data subject, be disclosed
rows.Mapping Rationale: This section prohibits disclosure of personal data unless consent or listed purpose/class conditions are met. Maps to P6-I4 because it creates a conditional cross-border transfer regime (a lawful pathway exists if conditions are satisfied), rather than an outright ban.
rows.Notes: Discovery: instrument is known but s. 8 is not among its recorded provisions (['1', '112', '113', '114', '12', '121', '129', '3', '4', '45', '5', '6', '9']). Modality: shall not (unless condition met); exceptions: section 39 subject-matter limitation; purpose originally specified at collection; purpose directly related to the original purpose; third party class specified in paragraph 7(1)(e)

rows.Verbatim Snippet: The processing of personal data by a data user shall be in compliance with the following Personal Data Protection Principles, namely— (a) the General Principle; (b) the Notice and Choice Principle; (c) the Disclosure Principle; (d) the Security Principle; (e) the Retention Principle; (f) the Data Integrity Principle; and (g) the Access Principle, as set out in sections 6, 7, 8, 9, 10, 11 and 12.
rows.Mapping Rationale: This section requires data users to comply with a horizontal set of personal-data-processing principles. Maps to P7-I1 because it establishes a comprehensive domestic data-protection framework rather than a sector-only rule.
rows.Notes: Discovery: master dataset already records s. 5 of this law. Modality: shall; exceptions: none

rows.Verbatim Snippet: There shall be exempted from the provisions of this Act personal data processed by an individual only for the purposes of that individual’s personal, family or household affairs, including recreational purposes.
rows.Mapping Rationale: This provision establishes a personal/household-use exemption. Maps to P7-I1 because it shows Act 709 is a general horizontal personal-data law, confirming a comprehensive framework exists rather than no framework.
rows.Notes: Discovery: master dataset already records s. 45 of this law. Modality: shall be exempted; exceptions: none

rows.Verbatim Snippet: (b) to implement and enforce the personal data protection laws, including the formulation of operational policies and procedures;
rows.Mapping Rationale: This section requires the Commissioner to implement and enforce personal data protection laws. Maps to P7-I1 because the Act shows a horizontal personal-data-protection framework exists in force, so the economy does not lack a comprehensive framework.
rows.Notes: Discovery: instrument is known but s. 48 is not among its recorded provisions (['1', '112', '113', '114', '12', '121', '129', '3', '4', '45', '5', '6', '9']). Modality: shall; exceptions: none

rows.Verbatim Snippet: the provisions of this Act shall be construed accordingly
rows.Mapping Rationale: This provision establishes interpretive coverage for data-access requests where a controller is deemed to hold personal data. Maps to P7-I1 because it is part of the horizontal personal data protection framework in Act 709, which means a comprehensive framework exists.
rows.Notes: Discovery: instrument is known but s. 30 is not among its recorded provisions (['1', '112', '113', '114', '12', '121', '129', '3', '4', '45', '5', '6', '9']). Modality: shall; exceptions: none

rows.Verbatim Snippet: (2) Subsection (1) shall not apply where— (a) the data subject has given his consent;
rows.Mapping Rationale: This provision permits processing of personal data subject to listed exceptions. Maps to P7-I1 because it sits within a horizontal personal-data-protection law, showing the economy has a comprehensive framework rather than no framework.

rows.Verbatim Snippet: to enhance the national cyber security by providing for the establishment of the National Cyber Security Committee, duties and powers of the Chief Executive of the National Cyber Security Agency, functions and duties of the national critical information infrastructure sector leads and national critical information infrastructure entities and the management of cyber security threats and cyber security incidents to national critical information infrastructures, to regulate the cyber security service providers through licensing

rows.Verbatim Snippet: Requirement not to hold or take records outside Australia
rows.Mapping Rationale: This section prohibits holding or taking records outside Australia. Maps to P6-I2 because it requires the record copy to remain domestically stored, while transfer may still occur if not accompanied by taking the records out.
rows.Notes: Discovery: master dataset already records s. 77 of this law. Modality: must not; exceptions: none

rows.Verbatim Snippet: must not: (a) hold the records, or take the records, outside Australia; or (b) process or handle the information relating to the records outside Australia;
rows.Mapping Rationale: This section prohibits taking/holding health record copies or processing related information outside Australia. Maps to P6-I2 because it requires the copy/records to remain stored domestically, while transfer/processing abroad is forbidden.
rows.Notes: Discovery: master dataset already records s. 77 of this law. Modality: must not; exceptions: none

rows.Verbatim Snippet: to hold and take such records outside Australia, provided that the records do not include: (i) personal information in relation to a healthcare recipient or a participant in the My Health Record system; or (ii) identifying information of an individual or entity;
rows.Mapping Rationale: This section permits the System Operator to hold a copy of records outside Australia. Maps to P6-I2 because it is a local-storage rule (copy may be stored abroad only if non-personal/identifying), not a transfer ban.
rows.Notes: Discovery: master dataset already records s. 77 of this law. Modality: authorised; exceptions: personal information in relation to a healthcare recipient or a participant in the My Health Record system; identifying information of an individual or entity
rows.Coverage: Sectoral (My Health Record system / health records)

rows.Verbatim Snippet: if a credit reporting body holds credit reporting information, the body must store the information: (a) either: (i) in Australia or an external Territory; or (ii) in accordance with any security requirements prescribed by the regulations for storing the information outside of Australia and the external Territories
rows.Mapping Rationale: This section requires a credit reporting body to store credit reporting information in Australia/external Territory or meet prescribed requirements for offshore storage. Maps to P6-I2 because it mandates domestic copy storage while still allowing transfer/offshore storage under conditions.
rows.Notes: Discovery: instrument is known but s. 20Q is not among its recorded provisions (none recorded). Modality: must; exceptions: paragraph (b) security requirements apply wherever the information is stored

rows.Verbatim Snippet: to promote the protection of the privacy of individuals with respect to their personal information;
rows.Mapping Rationale: This section establishes the Privacy Act's object of protecting personal information privacy. Maps to P7-I1 because it confirms a horizontal privacy/data-protection framework exists, so the economy does not lack one.
rows.Notes: Discovery: instrument is known but s. 2A is not among its recorded provisions (none recorded). Modality: establishes; exceptions: none

rows.Verbatim Snippet: This Act creates a framework for managing risks relating to critical infrastructure. The framework consists of the following: (a) the keeping of a register of information in relation to critical infrastructure assets
rows.Mapping Rationale: This Act establishes a framework for managing critical infrastructure risk, including cyber security incident notification and enhanced cyber security obligations. Maps to P7-I2 because it is a dedicated cybersecurity framework (at least sectoral for critical infrastructure), not merely scattered cl
rows.Notes: Discovery: instrument is known but s. 4 is not among its recorded provisions (['2014']). Modality: creates / requiring / allowing / imposing / setting up; exceptions: unless an exemption applies

rows.Verbatim Snippet: must keep, or cause to be kept, in accordance with section 187BA and for the period specified in section 187C
rows.Mapping Rationale: This section requires service providers to keep specified information/documents. Maps to P7-I3 because it sets a retention duty with a specified minimum period via section 187C, satisfying the legal test for minimum retention duration.
rows.Notes: Discovery: instrument is known but s. 187A is not among its recorded provisions (['10', '110', '2021', '317ZH', '474', '7']). Modality: must; exceptions: does not require keeping contents or substance of a communication; does not require keeping certain internet address/browser-history information; does not require keeping information/documents about communications carried by other services; does not require keeping information required to be deleted under section 99 of the Telecommunications Act 1997; does not require keeping certain location information

rows.Verbatim Snippet: Subject to subsection (3), the Attorney-General or the Director-General may, on request, authorise the Organisation to do one or more of the following things under the identified person warrant
rows.Mapping Rationale: This provision permits the Attorney-General or Director-General to authorize intelligence access/search under an identified person warrant.
